rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For An Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees, Costs And Penalties
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 5, 2018 line 1. PLAINTIFF CAITLIN FITTING's Motion For An Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees, Costs And Penalties GRANTED in part and denied in part. Motion for imposition of sanctions in the amount of $1,000 and for "other relief" per CCP 473(c)(1) is denied. Motion for recovery of reasonable compensatory legal fees is GRANTED. Karen Uchiyama shall remit to Plaintiff the sum of $5,575 representing reasonable amount of fees incurred in obtaining a prove-up judgment (executed on December 30, 2015). See Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816; Vanderkous v. Comley (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 111. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS GONZALEZ VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 5, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC's. DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to February 8, 2018 per stipulation and Order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR BARRERA VS. RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 5, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT RUSHMORE LOAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiffs have not provided sufficient facts to sustain a claim under the Business and Professions Code § 17200 because it is unclear whether Defendant's alleged conduct was unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent with respect to the loan modification requests. As to Plaintiff's second, third, and fourth causes of actions, Plaintiff does not provide sufficient facts to demonstrate that Defendant entered into agreement to review loan modification requests. Plaintiff's cause of action for injunctive relief is dependent on the survival other causes of action. = (501/REQ)
